top_stories.label: Top Stories
top_stories.ai_summary: It's a high-tension Friday: Tulsi Gabbard quit as US Director of National Intelligence to care for her husband, while Russia moved nuclear warheads into Belarus as part of joint drills, drawing sharp NATO warnings. Washington has paused a $14 billion arms package to Taiwan to focus on the Iran war, and Bolivia's capital is convulsed by protests demanding President Paz step down. The Ebola outbreak in DR Congo has spread far enough that India and the African Union postponed next week's summit in New Delhi.

stories.headline: Tulsi Gabbard resigns as US Director of National Intelligence
stories.summary: Gabbard told President Trump on Friday that she will step down as DNI effective 30 June, citing her husband Abraham Williams' diagnosis with a rare bone cancer. Principal Deputy Aaron Lukas will serve as acting DNI from 1 July. She is the fourth cabinet-level official to leave Trump's second term.

stories.headline: Russia transfers nuclear warheads to Belarus, NATO warns of 'devastating' response
stories.summary: Moscow confirmed it has moved tactical nuclear warheads for Iskander-M missile systems to a launcher site in Belarus, less than 200km from the Ukrainian border, as part of joint drills with 64,000 troops. NATO Secretary-General Mark Rutte said any nuclear strike would be met with a 'devastating' answer. Ukraine called the deployment 'an unprecedented challenge to global security'.

stories.headline: US pauses $14bn arms sale to Taiwan to prioritise Iran war
stories.summary: Acting Navy Secretary Hung Cao confirmed Washington is freezing the $14 billion Taiwan arms package because munitions and air-defence stocks are being redirected to the Gulf. The pause covers F-16 upgrades, NASAMS air-defence systems and Harpoon missiles. Taipei said it is seeking 'clarification' from the Pentagon.

stories.headline: Bolivia in deepening crisis as protests demand President Paz resign
stories.summary: Riot police clashed again with protesters in La Paz on Friday as the Bolivian Workers' Central, COB, peasant unions and miners kept the city under blockade for a 14th day. Year-on-year inflation hit 14% in April and at least four people, including a 12-year-old boy, have died because ambulances could not get through. President Paz reshuffled his cabinet on 21 May but demonstrators want him gone.

stories.headline: India-Africa Summit postponed as Ebola spreads to M23-held South Kivu
stories.summary: The African Union and India postponed next week's India-Africa Forum Summit in New Delhi after Ebola's spread into South Kivu — a region controlled by Rwanda-backed M23 rebels — where humanitarian access is severely limited. DR Congo's Health Ministry now reports more than 650 suspected cases and 160 deaths across 11 health zones.

stories.headline: SpaceX attempts maiden launch of Starship Version 3 as IPO looms
stories.summary: After a last-second scrub on Thursday from a stuck hydraulic pin, SpaceX is trying again Friday at 6:30pm ET to fly Starship Flight 12 — the debut of the larger Version 3 vehicle from Starbase Pad 2. Success would clear a major hurdle ahead of an expected SpaceX IPO that could value the company above $1 trillion.

stories.headline: Iran says US peace proposal has 'narrowed the gaps'
stories.summary: Tehran said Washington's latest one-page memorandum of understanding moved the two sides closer, though Iran's enriched uranium stockpile and US insistence on full Hormuz reopening remain sticking points. Trump earlier called the ceasefire 'on life support'; today's signal from Tehran is the most positive in two weeks.

singapore.label: Singapore
singapore.ai_summary: Cars and weather dominate the local agenda again: COE Category B is sitting just below a record S$130,000 and forecasters expect thundery afternoon showers right through the rest of May with highs near 35°C. There is a relieving update on the hantavirus front — both Singaporean returnees from the MV Hondius have completed quarantine after testing negative — and the collapsed Simba-M1 merger is still rippling through the telecom sector.

stories.headline: COE Cat B closes at S$129,501, Open Cat E hits S$130,000
stories.summary: Results from the 20 May bidding showed Category A at S$124,229 and Category B jumping S$3,265 to S$129,501, while the Open Cat E touched exactly S$130,000. Demand for larger cars far outstripped supply with 1,469 bids chasing just 869 Cat B certificates. Analysts expect premiums to stay elevated through 2026.

stories.headline: NEA: thundery showers most afternoons for rest of May, highs up to 35°C
stories.summary: The Meteorological Service Singapore's second-fortnight outlook says short-duration thundery showers will hit parts of the island on most afternoons, occasionally lasting into the evening. Pre-dawn Sumatra squalls remain possible. Daily highs are forecast at 33-34°C with a few days touching 35°C.

stories.headline: Both Singapore residents from MV Hondius cleared after hantavirus tests
stories.summary: The CDA confirmed that both Singaporean residents who returned from the MV Hondius cruise have tested negative for hantavirus, including the Andes strain. They remained under phone surveillance through the 45-day incubation window. CDA continues to assess public risk to Singapore as low.

stories.headline: S$1.43 billion Simba-M1 acquisition collapses after IMDA conditions miss
stories.summary: The Simba-led acquisition of M1 has been terminated because key deal conditions were not satisfied by the 21 May deadline, ending months of IMDA review. Singapore's four-telco market structure is preserved for now. Industry watchers expect the failure to revive consolidation talks in 12-18 months.

stories.headline: 80 summonses issued in latest LTA crackdown on noisy, smoky cars
stories.summary: LTA and police issued 80 summonses in joint enforcement targeting excessive vehicular emissions and noise across the island, Mothership reported. Offenders face up to S$5,000 in fines plus mandatory inspection. The blitz follows mounting resident complaints about modified exhausts in residential estates.

wars.label: Wars & Conflicts
wars.ai_summary: Friday was the most escalatory day of the week. Russia openly moved nuclear warheads to Belarusian launch sites under cover of Iskander drills, while Trump's 72-hour Ukraine truce has collapsed back into nightly drone barrages. In the Gulf, the US-Iran ceasefire is fragile but Iran says Washington's latest peace text 'narrowed the gaps'. Israel and Lebanon extended their own truce by 45 days, but the 657-death toll since April shows how brittle that calm really is.

theatres.name: Russia–Ukraine War
theatres.status: Active — Russia moves nukes to Belarus, drone war intensifies

timeline.event: Russia launches full-scale invasion of Ukraine
Russia gains net 1,585 sq miles (~0.7% of Ukraine) over 12 months to May 2026
Russia launches 8,000+ drones in a single month, a record high
Trump brokers 72-hour ceasefire from 9 May, both sides accuse the other of breaching
Truce expires; Zelenskyy reports over 200 Russian drones overnight
Four-week reversal: Russia loses net 69 sq miles to Ukrainian counter-attacks
Russia–Belarus 'strategic nuclear' drills begin: 64,000 troops, 7,800 platforms
Russia delivers nuclear warheads to Belarus under cover of Iskander-M drills
NATO Secretary-General Mark Rutte warns Moscow of 'devastating' response to any nuclear use

theatres.name: US/Israel–Iran War (Strait of Hormuz)
theatres.status: Fragile ceasefire — sporadic clashes, peace text under review

timeline.event: US and Israel launch air war on Iran; assassinate Supreme Leader Khamenei
Iran declares Strait of Hormuz 'closed', commercial traffic falls 90%+
Pakistan brokers two-week ceasefire between US, Israel and Iran
Israel-Lebanon ceasefire begins on a parallel track
US says offensive phase over; Iran says it won't surrender Hormuz
US and Iranian forces clash inside Strait of Hormuz, ceasefire wobbles
Iran's IRGC coordinates passage of 26 vessels through Hormuz in 24 hours
Iran says new US one-page MoU has 'narrowed the gaps'
US pauses $14bn Taiwan arms package, redirecting munitions to the Gulf
10-day Israel-Lebanon ceasefire begins, brokered by Washington
Israel issues forced evacuation orders in southern Lebanon, raising fears of collapse
Third round of Washington talks between Israeli, Lebanese and US delegations
Israeli strike on Harouf kills at least six, including three paramedics
Ceasefire extended by 45 days; Hezbollah disarmament still unresolved
Lebanese health ministry: 657 killed in Israeli strikes since the April ceasefire began

hantavirus.label: Hantavirus Outbreak
hantavirus.ai_summary: The MV Hondius Andes hantavirus cluster has stabilised at 11 cases and three deaths, with the ship now docked in Rotterdam for sanitation. Singapore's two returnees are fully cleared. ECDC and WHO still rate the public risk as very low — the harder story this week is the Bundibugyo Ebola outbreak in DR Congo and Uganda, now declared a global health emergency.

stories.headline: MV Hondius outbreak: 11 cases, 3 deaths, ship docked for sanitation
stories.summary: As of 21 May, the ECDC reports 11 hantavirus cases (9 confirmed, 2 probable) and three deaths — two confirmed caused by Andes hantavirus. The Hondius has been docked in Rotterdam since 18 May undergoing ship sanitation. Former passengers remain hospitalised or in quarantine across 12 countries.

stories.headline: Singapore residents fully cleared, public risk stays low
stories.summary: The two Singaporean residents who travelled on the Hondius tested negative through multiple rounds at NCID. They completed the 45-day phone-surveillance window. CDA continues to monitor incoming travellers and says local risk remains low.

stories.headline: WHO declares DR Congo–Uganda Bundibugyo Ebola outbreak a global emergency
stories.summary: The IHR Emergency Committee on 17 May determined the Bundibugyo Ebola outbreak a Public Health Emergency of International Concern. As of 22 May, DR Congo reports 650+ suspected cases and 160 deaths across 11 health zones, with confirmed cases in Kinshasa and Kampala. No licensed vaccine exists for the Bundibugyo strain.

stories.headline: CDC mobilises full international response to Ebola outbreak
stories.summary: The US CDC has activated its Emergency Operations Center and deployed a multidisciplinary team to DR Congo and Uganda. Airport screening for travellers from affected areas is being scaled up at US ports of entry. CDC says the risk to the US public remains 'low'.

cybersecurity.label: CyberSecurity
cybersecurity.ai_summary: It's a brutal week for defenders. CISA added two more actively exploited bugs to the KEV catalog — a Trend Micro Apex One traversal flaw and Microsoft Defender's privilege-escalation hole — both with public exploit code. Cisco's SD-WAN max-severity CVSS-10 zero-day is being actively used by a sophisticated group (UAT-8616), Drupal is begging admins to patch a 'highly critical' SQL-injection bug, and Ubiquiti dropped three separate CVSS-10 fixes for UniFi OS. Patch tonight, not tomorrow.

stories.headline: Cisco patches max-severity SD-WAN zero-day exploited by UAT-8616
stories.summary: Cisco disclosed CVE-2026-20182, a perfect 10.0 authentication bypass in Catalyst SD-WAN that lets attackers establish themselves as trusted peers and gain admin privileges remotely. The flaw is configuration-independent and is being actively exploited by a 'highly sophisticated' threat actor dubbed UAT-8616. No workarounds — patch immediately.

stories.headline: Microsoft patches Defender zero-day with public exploit code
stories.summary: CVE-2026-41091 is a privilege-escalation flaw in the Microsoft Malware Protection Engine that lets a low-privileged local attacker reach SYSTEM. It is being exploited in the wild and exploit code is publicly available. CISA added it to KEV on 20 May; federal agencies must patch by 3 June.

stories.headline: Trend Micro Apex One directory-traversal flaw added to CISA KEV
stories.summary: Trend Micro confirmed CVE-2026-34926, a directory traversal in Apex One on-premise that lets an attacker with admin access modify a key table to inject malicious code that is pushed to managed agents. TrendAI says it has seen at least one in-the-wild exploitation attempt. CISA mandated federal patching by 4 June.

stories.headline: Drupal core 'highly critical' SQL injection threatens PostgreSQL sites
stories.summary: Drupal SA-CORE-2026-004 / CVE-2026-9082 is an anonymous SQL-injection bug in Drupal core's database abstraction layer that affects every site running PostgreSQL on Drupal 8.9.0 or later. Successful exploitation can lead to data theft, privilege escalation and RCE. Drupal scored the bug 20/25 — its 'highly critical' tier — and active exploitation attempts are already being seen.

stories.headline: Ubiquiti drops triple CVSS-10 fixes for UniFi OS
stories.summary: Ubiquiti released emergency updates for five UniFi OS flaws — three of which rate a perfect CVSS 10.0. CVE-2026-34908 (improper access control), CVE-2026-34909 (path traversal) and CVE-2026-34910 (command injection) all allow unauthenticated remote takeover of UDM, UNVR, UCG and UNAS appliances. Patch to UniFi OS Server 5.0.8 / device firmware 5.1.12.

stories.headline: First AI-discovered zero-day exploited in the wild: 2FA bypass
stories.summary: Google's threat intel team disclosed what it calls the first confirmed instance of attackers using a frontier AI model to discover and weaponise a zero-day — a 2FA bypass in an open-source admin tool. Google said it disrupted the campaign before mass exploitation; the spyware industry is reportedly studying the technique closely.

stories.headline: MiniPlasma Windows zero-day enables SYSTEM on fully-patched 11
stories.summary: Researchers disclosed 'MiniPlasma', a privilege-escalation flaw in cldflt.sys (the Windows Cloud Files Mini Filter Driver) that reliably opens a SYSTEM-level cmd.exe on Windows 11 systems running the May 2026 patch. Microsoft is preparing an out-of-band fix; in the meantime, the bug bypasses Defender.
